claim: John Cullen, a former commercial pilot and independent investigator, has publicly argued that the round which killed Charlie Kirk was never recovered and therefore has no ballistic match to the bolt-action .30-06 rifle found in the wooded area near Utah Valley University. Cullen further argues, in his ongoing YouTube and X publications on the case, that the publicly available audio and video evidence are inconsistent with a single rooftop shooter at 142 yards.

sources.label: John Cullen on X — “The round that shot Charlie Kirk was not recovered” (@I_Am_JohnCullen)
John Cullen — “Hypothetically Speaking: Who Shot Charlie Kirk, and, From Where?” (YouTube)
BIBLE Companion / @bible.app_, Instagram reel DTQb5oiAMHb (Jan 8, 2026) — surfacing source

notes: Cullen's central factual claim — that no recovered round has been ballistically matched to the recovered rifle — is testable against the Utah County Attorney's charging document and the FBI's public statements. As of the charging-document filing in September 2025, the prosecution's case rests on DNA evidence on the rifle, the towel, and the trigger (see 2025-kirk-002); the charging document does not reference a recovered projectile. Whether that absence is forensically significant is the contested question. This record documents the public claim and the cited primary sources; readers can compare against 2025-kirk-002 directly.
